106.1.title: VCE Vocational Major and Victorian Pathways Certificate Provision
106.1.summary: This policy outlines the provision expectation for the Victorian Certificate of Education (VCE) Vocational Major (VM) and Victorian Pathways Certificate (VPC) for Victorian government secondary schools. The VCE VM is a 2-year vocational and applied learning program that enables students to transition into apprenticeships, traineeships, further education and training and university (via non-ATAR pathways).

106.5.title: Audits, Reviews and Assurance Programs in Schools
106.5.summary: This page provides an overview of the department’s regular audits, reviews and other assurance activities held in Victorian government schools. The department conducts a number of audits, reviews and other assurance activities in Victorian government schools.

106.8.title: Canteens
106.8.summary: This policy directs schools to information about the requirements for school canteens. This page links schools to information about: When establishing and managing a canteen, schools must comply with:

106.9.title: Career Education Funding
106.9.summary: This policy outlines the requirements for schools in receipt of career education funding. Career education funding supports Victorian government secondary schools, including specialist schools and English language schools, to supplement the delivery of career education activities to all students in Years 7 to 12.

107.3.title: Private Vehicle Use
107.3.summary: The purpose of this policy is to ensure schools meet safety and legal requirements when using private vehicles for official business. Schools must not direct staff members to transport other staff, students or equipment.

107.4.title: Procuring Low-value Construction Works or Services
107.4.summary: This policy sets out actions that schools must take to procure construction works or services valued between $5,000 and $150,000 (including GST). for instructions on how to procure construction contracts valued between $5,000 and $150,000 (including GST) in compliance with this policy.

108.title: Student Resource Package – Targeted Initiatives
108.summary: This policy provides information about how funding is allocated to schools through the Student Resource Package (SRP) for targeted initiatives. Funding for ‘targeted initiatives’ is one of 3 categories of funding provided for by the SRP.

chapters.chapter_title: Flexible work for school leaders initiative (Reference 151)
Flexible Work for Classroom Teachers initiative (Reference 154)

108.2.title: Vehicles (Excluding Buses) – Owned or Hired by a School
108.2.summary: This policy sets out the requirements for vehicles owned or hired by a school, excluding buses. Schools are permitted to own or hire vehicles seating up to 9 adults (including the driver) for school activities.

109.5.title: Leave — Bushfire, Flood or Other Severe Weather Event
109.5.summary: As part of the department’s duty of care principals or managers need to ensure they act sensitively towards employees whose circumstances require them to be absent from the school or workplace as a result of bushfire, flood or other severe weather event. Employees are affected in many ways either directly or indirectly, including through the loss of family or friends or through the loss or damage to their homes and property.

110.2.title: Access to Health Information — Employees
110.2.summary: The Health Records Act 2001 provides a legally enforceable right for people to access health information held about them in Victoria. Employees also have a right to seek access under the Freedom of Information Act 1982 to information about them including medical reports and referral letters held at the school and by the Department.

132.5.title: Volunteers in Schools
132.5.summary: The purpose of this policy is to explain the legal rights of volunteer school workers and the responsibilities of schools in managing volunteers. is available on the School Policy Templates Portal.
